name: Block Restricted Data on USB
description: Blocks file transfers to USB drives when content is classified as Restricted (confidence >= 80%)

config.description: Block highly sensitive data from being copied to USB drives

name: Block Confidential Data on USB
description: Blocks file transfers to USB drives when content is classified as Confidential (confidence >= 60%)

config.description: Block confidential data from being copied to USB drives

name: Alert on Internal Data USB Transfer
description: Generates alert when Internal-classified data is transferred to USB (does not block)

config.description: Alert when internal data is copied to USB drives

name: Block High-Confidence Sensitive USB Transfers
description: Blocks USB transfers of content classified Confidential/Restricted with high confidence. REQUIRES a blocking classification_level, not just a score: score alone fires on ordinary documents (a contact sheet of 3 emails once scored 0.84 while correctly classifying Internal). The level is the decision; the score only grades our confidence in it.

config.description: Block USB transfers with high classification confidence regardless of level

name: Cloud Upload — Block Sensitive
description: Blocks uploads to cloud storage when the content classifies Confidential or Restricted.

config.description: Block sensitive data leaving via cloud upload

alert.message: Sensitive data upload to cloud blocked

name: Email Send — Block Sensitive Attachments
description: Rejects outbound mail (550 at DATA) whose body or attachments classify Confidential or Restricted.

config.description: Block sensitive data leaving via email

name: Cloud Upload — Alert Internal
description: Alerts (does not block) when Internal-classified data is uploaded to cloud storage.

config.description: Visibility over Internal data going to cloud

name: Email Send — Alert Internal
description: Alerts (does not block) when Internal-classified data is emailed out.

config.description: Visibility over Internal data going out by email

name: Block Uninspectable Content (all channels)
description: Blocks content that SHOULD have been readable but wasn't: encrypted or opaque archives, corrupt/renamed office files, legacy .doc/.xls/.ppt, images that could not be OCR'd. 'We couldn't inspect it' must never mean 'it's clean', or encrypting a file becomes a one-step bypass. Deliberately does NOT fire on files that simply contain no text (photos, video, installers, disk images) — those report no_text_content and are left alone, because blocking them is a pure false positive.

config.description: Refuse documents that could not be inspected

name: Block Oversized Uninspected Files
description: Blocks files too large to inspect (over the agent's 25MB cap, or whose text outran the scan budget). This closes the padding bypass: without it, padding a secret past the cap makes it skip inspection entirely and pass. TRADE-OFF: this also blocks large legitimate files such as videos and disk images, which are genuinely uninspectable. If big-file workflows matter more than that bypass, change this policy's action from block to alert — it is intentionally separate from 'Block Uninspectable Content' so you can tune the two independently.

config.description: Refuse files that were never inspected because they were too large
